policy_statement: The National Adaptation Plan (NAP) Process will serve as a 10-year blueprint (2020–2030) guiding adaptation planning and implementation, with a review around 2029 and a new framework developed for 2031–2040.
verbatim_text: The NAP Process (this  document)  will  serve  as  a  blueprint  to  guide  the  overall  process  and  provide structure.  This NAP Process will be in effect for ten years (2020-2030), during which time there will be ongoing monitoring and evaluation,  in approximately 2029, this first period  of  implementation  will be reviewed, and a new framework will be developed to guide the period from 2031-2040.
extraction_rationale: Defines an official NAP Process implementation timeframe (2020–2030) plus scheduled review and development of a subsequent framework—clear planned government action with deadlines.

policy_statement: Liberia’s NAP Process (2020–2030) will reduce vulnerability and build adaptive capacity/resilience, and facilitate integration of climate change adaptation into fiscal, regulatory, and development policies, programs, and activities.
verbatim_text:
The  road  map  provides  a  guideline  for  implementing the NAP process in Liberia and working in the short, medium, and long term. The objectives of the NAP process are:

- i. to  reduce  vulnerability  to  the  adverse  impacts  of  climate  change  by  building  adaptive capacity and resilience; and,
- ii. to  facilitate  the  integration  of  climate  change  adaptation  into  fiscal,  regulatory,  and development policies, programs, and activities (UNFCCC, 2012).
extraction_rationale: States explicit objectives of the national NAP process, including integration into fiscal/regulatory/development policy domains (a concrete governance commitment).

policy_statement: The GCF-funded NAP project will focus on four outputs: strengthening institutional frameworks/coordination for NAP implementation, expanding the knowledge base for scaling up adaptation, building capacity to mainstream adaptation into planning and budgeting, and formulating financing mechanisms for scaling up adaptation.
verbatim_text:
The  activities  in  this  project  focus  on  four outputs:

- i. Strengthening institutional frameworks and coordination for implementation of the NAPs.
- ii. Expansion of the knowledge base for scaling up adaptation.
- iii. Building  capacity  for  mainstreaming  climate  change  adaptation  into  planning  and budgeting processes; and
- iv. systems formulation of financing mechanisms for scaling up adaptation (including public, private, national, and international)
extraction_rationale: Enumerates specific, structured project outputs to be delivered under a funded initiative—clear interventions rather than context.

policy_statement: NAP Process Objective 1 will implement actions to improve climate projections and adaptation technology planning, including developing downscaled projections and an adaptation technology roadmap.
verbatim_text:
Objective 1: Provide a framework and procedures for sharing information of scientific, technical, and  traditional  knowledge  on  climate  change  risk  management  and  develop  capacity-building measures

| Goal 1.1 Develop and implement a strategy to enhance the quality of the climatic projections |
...
| Initiatives | 1. Identify priority sectors and technologies for adaptation 2. Develop downscaled projections for potential future climate conditions based on the most recent global climate model (GCM) outputs |
...
| Goal 1.2 Develop a plan of action on technology needs for adaptation |
...
| Initiatives | 1. Conduct mapping and evaluation of technology needs for adaptation through partnerships to determine national key player 2. Develop a roadmap to identify priority technologies. |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Contains concrete deliverables (strategy for projections, downscaled projections, technology needs mapping, roadmap) under NAP objectives.

policy_statement: Liberia will prepare and deploy an online climate data integration system for monitoring, dissemination, and awareness-raising on climate change impacts, using platforms such as the CCKS/EKMS managed by the EPA.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 1.3 Prepare and deploy an online data integration system for monitoring, dissemination, and awareness-raising on the impacts of climate change |
| Initiatives | Share adaptation data on the Climate Change Knowledge Sharing Platform (CCKS) or the EKMS or consider an initiative: developing an information management architecture to disseminate climate change to various stakeholder groups effectively. The CCKS or EMKS is a web sharing platform managed by the EPA... |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to deploy an online system/platform for monitoring and dissemination—specific intervention.

policy_statement: Liberia will formulate a capacity-building strategy for adaptation including awareness/public mobilization, training professionals and vulnerable groups, and strengthening institutions and research for adaptation knowledge dissemination.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 2.1. Formulate a capacity-building strategy for adaptation for various target public agencies, CSOs, NGOs |
| Initiatives | 1. Conduct awareness building and public mobilization activities 2. Conduct a capacity building program for professionals and active leaders in strategic areas and among the more vulnerable group 3. Promote the production and dissemination of knowledge on adaptation by strengthening institution and research |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Lists defined initiatives under a stated goal to build capacity—concrete actions and program elements.

policy_statement: Liberia will deploy a NAP monitoring and evaluation (M&E) system integrating adaptation into monitoring systems and including adaptation actions of government and sectors.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 2.2. Deployment of NAP monitoring and evaluation (M &E)system |
| Initiatives | 1. M&Esystem for adaptation integrated into the monitoring system 2. Include the actions of adaptation of GoL, sectors |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Clear system deployment commitment with stated integration and scope.

policy_statement: Liberia will draft and initiate adaptation strategies by forming an inter-governmental working group and supporting formulation and preparation of an adaptation strategy with methodologies, inputs, and training.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 2.3. Drafting and initiation of adaptation strategies |
| Initiatives | 1. Form an inter-governmental working group 2. Support formulation of an adaptation strategy, with inputs of knowledge, methodologies, and training 3. Prepare the strategy |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Specifies institutional setup (working group) and concrete steps to produce an adaptation strategy.

policy_statement: Liberia will develop and deploy sectoral climate risk and vulnerability assessments, including sector-specific monitoring methodologies and improved modeling/estimation methods for climate change risk.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 3.1. Develop and deploy sectoral risk vulnerability assessment |
| Initiatives | 1. Develop sectoral risk and vulnerability monitoring methodology for each sector 2. Enhance methods for modeling and estimation of climate change risk |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Commits to develop/deploy assessments and methodologies—specific intervention to support adaptation planning.

policy_statement: Liberia will establish a climate intelligence center at the EPA, including support systems for spatial/integrated analysis and an agriculture communication and early-warning component.
verbatim_text:
| Goal 3.2. Establish a center for climatic intelligence for all sectors at the EPA |
| Initiatives | 1. Develop a support system for spatial and integrated analysis 2. Create the climate intelligence center for Agriculture communication and early-warning |
section_header: 1.4. Goals and Objectives of Liberia's NAP Process
extraction_rationale: Institution-building action (establish a center) with defined components and location (EPA).

policy_statement: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
verbatim_text:
Accordingly, the NAP process is divided into six strategies. The strategies discuss each sector's principal vulnerabilities and management from a climate change perspective and present current guidelines for implementing adaptation measures to increase climate resilience.

## 5.2. Proposed sectors adaptation strategies
extraction_rationale: Introduces a structured set of sector strategies with multiple detailed actions underneath, functioning as an umbrella (parent) for the sector-specific sub-policies extracted next.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen Ministry of Agriculture capacity; improve integrated pest/disease/weed management and resistant varieties; assess crop vulnerability/suitability by agro-ecological zones; and enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructure to raise farmer productivity.
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Ministry of Agriculture, including training of experts, logistics, and use of technology for the management of the sector.
• Improve the effectiveness of pest, disease, and weed management practices through the broader use of integrated pest and pathogen management...
• Assess crops vulnerability and suitability (cropping pattern) for different Agroecological zones.
• Enhance climate-proof agro-infrastructural systems that strengthen the capacity of local farmers to increase productivity.
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Bullet-listed concrete interventions under the agriculture adaptation strategy table; clearly actionable capacity/infrastructure and management measures.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: support communities via indigenous knowledge dissemination, insurance schemes, early warning/early action systems, vaccination campaigns and disease control to cope with climate variability.
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Support communities in livestock and crop sectors by inventorying and disseminating indigenous knowledge, establishing and/or strengthening insurance schemes, early warning, early action system, vaccination campaign, disease control, etc., to cope with the stress based on climate variability.
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Specifies multiple concrete support mechanisms (insurance, EWS/EAS, vaccination, disease control) for adaptation.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: develop and introduce integrated soil fertility management, water harvesting, and conservation techniques to improve soil fertility and cope with aridity, waterlogging and floods.
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Develop and introduce a diverse range of integrated Soil fertility management (SFM), water harvesting, and conservation techniques to farmers as a sustainable means of improving soil fertility, intensifying agricultural production, and coping with extreme conditions (aridity, waterlogging, flood, etc.)
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Concrete practice/technology package to be developed and introduced to farmers.

policy_statement: Agriculture adaptation strategy: strengthen CARI capacity for climate-smart agriculture, including establishing seed banks and improving soil management, crop diversification, and livestock breeds; and develop climate-resilient crops including drought-resistant and flood-tolerant varieties and gene banks.
verbatim_text:
Table 7: Adaptation strategies of the agricultural sector

• Strengthen the Central Agricultural Research Institute (CARI) capacity for research development of climate smart agriculture initiates in Liberia, including the setting up of seed banks and soil management, crop diversification... improved livestock breeds, etc.
• Develop climate-resilient crops, promote drought - resistant, flood-tolerant, and establishing gene bank of climate-resilient varieties of indigenous food crops (NDC, 2015)
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Institutional strengthening plus specific deliverables (seed banks/gene banks; resilient varieties) are clear adaptation actions.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: develop and implement coastal zone policy/design/management plan; develop an integrated coastal management plan and early warning system with training; promote disaster risk management and preparedness; rehabilitate and protect wetlands and mangroves; and climate-proof new coastal infrastructure investments.
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• develop and implement coastal zone policy, design, and management plan(NDC, 2015)
• Develop an integrated management plan for coastal zone management as well as an early warning system that includes training and capacity development for coastal management and monitoring
• Promote and implement disaster risk management in general (especially disaster preparedness).
• Support the rehabilitation and protection of wetlands and mangroves...
• Develop and implement a program for climate-proofing new investments in infrastructure (roads, sewers, water supplies, and other infrastructure) in coastal settlements and rural areas...
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: A suite of concrete coastal adaptation measures, including planning instruments, EWS, DRM, ecosystem protection, and infrastructure climate-proofing.

policy_statement: Coastal adaptation strategy: design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate the public on climate change-related coastal changes, challenges, and adaptation options.
verbatim_text:
Table 8: Adaptation strategies of the coastal sector

• Design and implement a strategic communication action plan to inform and educate people about changes and challenges associated with coastal areas related to climate change and how they can adapt to cope with these changes and challenges.
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Specific planned intervention (communication action plan) with clear purpose and scope.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: establish and promote a national solar energy program and other energy-efficient lighting technologies, including provision of energy-efficient technologies for schools, public institutions, and households.
verbatim_text:
Table 9: Adaptation strategies of the energy sector

• Establish and promote a robust national program on solar energy (e.g., hybrid systems, installation of solar panels, promotion of solar street lighting, etc.) and other energy-efficient lighting technologies.
• Support the provision of energy-efficient technologies such as energy-efficient bulbs to provide power and lighting for schools and other public institutions and households...
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Concrete energy adaptation/transition actions (solar program, efficiency tech deployment) explicitly proposed as strategy measures.

policy_statement: Energy adaptation strategy: promote energy plantations to reduce pressure on natural forests, regulate sustainable biomass energy use, support community-based off-grid/mini-grids, conserve/protect water catchments for hydropower and municipal supply, and prevent sedimentation affecting hydropower generation.
verbatim_text:
Table 9: Adaptation strategies of the energy sector

• Support the promotion and implementation of energy plantation schemes to minimize natural forest pressure and reduce energy stress.
• Develop a system to regulate the sustainable use of biomass energy.
• Promote and support the development and utilization of community-based off- grid/mini-grids.
• Conservation and protection of water catchments, including around hydro- power and municipal water supply sources.
• Prevent sedimentation that could hinder the production of energy in hydroelectric facilities
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Multiple specific interventions for resilience and sustainability in the energy sector are explicitly listed.

policy_statement: Fisheries adaptation strategy: strengthen capacity of fisheries authorities for research/monitoring/enforcement; support artisanal fishing communities with training, gear and alternative livelihoods; establish monitoring/reporting/verification and adaptive management; support research on climate pressures; identify/map/protect valuable fisheries areas including marine protected areas; reduce external stressors via regulatory measures; integrate fisheries into national adaptation and food security policies; establish early warning and information/communication networks; and regulate fishing to prevent overexploitation.
verbatim_text:
Table 10: Adaptation strategies of the fisheries sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the Bureau of National Fisheries... for research monitoring and enforcement.
• Invest in and support artisanal fishing communities, including training, fishing gear, and alternative livelihoods.
• Set up robust monitoring, reporting, and verification system...
• Identify, map, and protect areas valuable for fisheries... including the setting up of marine protected areas.
• ...instituting actions and regulatory measures to reduce land-based sources of pollution... and destructive fishing practices...
• Integrate fisheries fully into climate change adaptation and food security policies at the national level...
• Support the establishment of early warning systems...
• ...Regulate fishing practices to prevent overexploitation and fishing in restricted areas (NAPA, 2008)
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Comprehensive list of concrete governance, capacity, protection, and regulatory measures presented as sector adaptation strategies.

policy_statement: Forestry adaptation strategy: strengthen FDA capacity and logistics; implement sustainable/alternative livelihoods for forest-dependent communities; promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction; establish comprehensive forest (including NTFPs) monitoring; implement reforestation/afforestation in degraded areas; map and manage forest water catchments; consolidate protected area networks with landscape connectivity; strengthen coordination with other agencies; enforce anti-poaching regulations and a wildlife code of ethics; and implement communication strategies to raise awareness on climate impacts and adaptation.
verbatim_text:
Table 31: Adaptation strategies of the forestry sector

• Strengthen the capacity of the FDA, including training of experts and logistics for forest management
• Implement sustainable and, where applicable alternative livelihood initiatives for forest-dependent communities...
• Promote community forest activities beyond timber extraction...
• Establish a comprehensive monitoring system for forest resources...
• Implement reforestation and afforestation activities in degraded areas...
• Identify and map for proper management of water catchment areas in forests...
• Promote the consolidation of protected area network...
• Enforce regulations related to illegal hunting...
• Develop and implement a communication strategy to increase the awareness...
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Explicit sector strategy with multiple specific interventions including capacity, monitoring, restoration, protected areas, enforcement, and outreach.

policy_statement: Waste management adaptation/mitigation strategy: strengthen community and institutional capacity for integrated waste management; develop an integrated waste management strategy prioritizing waste prevention and low-GHG technologies compatible with methane capture for electricity; promote PPP financing for waste infrastructure; implement composting to convert urban waste into inputs for agriculture; and develop landfills for major cities including methane recovery and power generation using CDM and NAMAs.
verbatim_text:
Table 42: Adaptation strategies of the waste management sector

• Strengthen capacity at the community and institutional level for integrated waste management.
• Develop an integrated waste management strategy and system for all types of waste, assigning priority to prevent waste generation with nationally appropriate low greenhouse gas emission technologies... compatible with methane capture and use for electricity generation.
• Promote private-public partnership (PPP)...
• Design and implement a system to run urban waste into input for agricultural production through composting...
• Develop landfills for all major cities and use the Clean Development Mechanism (CDM) and Nationally Appropriate Mitigation Actions (NAMAs)to develop methane recovery and power generation projects in landfills.
parent_policy_name: Liberia will implement proposed adaptation strategies through sectoral strategies under the NAP Process for priority sectors (agriculture, coastal zones, forestry, energy, waste management, fisheries) and cross-cutting sectors (water resources, biodiversity).
extraction_rationale: Lists concrete policy/program actions with specific mechanisms (PPP, composting, landfills, methane capture, CDM/NAMAs).

policy_statement: Water resources cross-cutting adaptation action will fast-track mainstreaming climate change into water resources management and establish a surveillance team on water resource vulnerability.
verbatim_text:
Table 13: Adaptation Strategy-Cross-Cutting Water Resources

| Main Action | This sector hopes to deliver the following results: • Fast-tracking the implementation on the mainstreaming of climate change into water resources management • Establishing a surveillance team on water resources vulnerability |
extraction_rationale: Concrete cross-cutting actions stated as deliverable results (mainstreaming + surveillance team).

policy_statement: Biodiversity cross-cutting adaptation action will establish a working group to scope and prioritize ecosystem-based adaptation (EbA) measures, assess climate change impacts on biodiversity/ecosystems, and fast-track integration of EbA approaches into Liberia’s overall adaptation response.
verbatim_text:
Table 14: Adaptation Strategy - Cross-Cutting Biodiversity

| Main Action | This sector hopes to deliver the following results through the establishment of a working group to • conduct a scoping study to identify and prioritize areas for Eba measures... • Conduct (nationwide/regional) assessmentson potential climate change impacts on biodiversity and ecosystems.' • Fast track the integration of Ecosystem-based Adaptation (EbA) approaches into Liberia's overall adaptation response to climate change |
extraction_rationale: Defines institutional mechanism (working group) and specific tasks (scoping study, assessments, fast-track integration).

policy_statement: Institutional arrangements for NAP implementation will include a National Climate Change Steering Committee (NCCSC), EPA as lead agency, and the National Climate Change Secretariat (NCCS) with defined roles for validation, coordination, reporting, and recordkeeping.
verbatim_text:
Table 15: National level actor function

NCCSC: "Validate (bi-annual) and secure government support for the implementation of the NAP Process... Report bi-annual on the progress of the NAP Process to the President..."

EPA: "The EPA shall be the led agency on the implementation of the NAP Process - Coordinate the NAP Process consultation..."

NCCS: "Track the progress on the implementation NAPProcess... Maintain full records..."
extraction_rationale: Sets out a formal governance structure with explicit responsibilities and reporting cadence (bi-annual validation/reporting).

policy_statement: GoL, through the EPA, will form Local Adaptation Plan Committees to support implementation of the NAP Process and develop local-level adaptive capacity-building to facilitate communities.
verbatim_text: Therefore, it is imperative for GoL through the EPA to engage in forming Local Adaptation Plan Committee that will aid the national level in implementing the NAP Process. The Local Adaptation Plan Committee will also develop local-level adaptive capacity-building, and experiential facilitate the local community.
extraction_rationale: Explicit institutional action: forming local committees with defined purpose to implement and build capacity.

policy_statement: The EPA and Ministry of Internal Affairs will coordinate with subnational levels for NAP implementation, with EPA providing experts to work with districts and villages/communities, and adaptation strategy projects implemented at subnational level.
verbatim_text: In collaboration with the Environmental Protection Agency, the Ministry of Internal Affairs will play the lead role in coordinating with the government's subnational levels. The EPA will provide experts that will work with the communities at the district and village or community... The implementation of the adaptation strategies projects should be at the subnational (districts/villages).
extraction_rationale: Defines specific roles and implementation level for projects (districts/villages), indicating an operational commitment.

policy_statement: GoL through the EPA will establish mechanisms to increase access to climate finance (including the Green Climate Fund) and will develop a pipeline of investment-grade adaptation projects/programmes to attract partner and private sector finance.
verbatim_text: GoL through the EPA needs to establish mechanisms to help increase access to climate finance, including the Green Climate Fund, and improve coordination and reporting on climate finance. This NAP envisages  that  the  government's  adaptation  actions  do  not,  yet  finance  will  attract  financing from development  partners  and  the  private  sector  by  developing  a  pipeline  of  adaptation  investment-grade projects and programme.
extraction_rationale: Commits to establishing finance access mechanisms and a project pipeline—clear resource mobilization actions.

policy_statement: EPA, with MFDP, will establish a Climate Public Expenditure and Institutional Review (CPEIR) and a climate finance tracking system to identify existing climate expenditures and on-budget entry points for incorporating climate change into the national budget.
verbatim_text: However, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) in partnership with the Ministry of Finance and Development  Planning  (MFDP)  will  establish  the  climate  public  expenditure  and institutional  review (CPEIR) and Climate finance tracking system. The CPEIR will identify existing expenditures on climate change and will help to identify on-budget entry points where climate change could be incorporated into the national budget.
extraction_rationale: Specific institutional mechanisms to be established (CPEIR and tracking system) with clear intended function.

policy_statement: Each ministry/agency will conduct internal monitoring of NAP implementation and submit quarterly and annual monitoring reports to the EPA; the EPA will provide technical support and compile national quarterly and annual summary reports for submission to national planning and higher bodies.
verbatim_text:
Each ministry or  agency  is  supposed  to  conduct  internal  monitoring  processes  during  the  entire  NAP implementation  process...
They  will  prepare  periodic  monitoring  reports  (quarter  and  annual  reports)... and submit them to the EPA. EPA will organize and provide technical backup and capacity building on the monitoring process. EPA will also prepare aggregate quarterly and  annual national summary reports... and  submit  them  to  the  Ministry  responsible  for National Planning and the higher body assigned by the President's office...
extraction_rationale: Defines concrete reporting requirements, frequency (quarterly/annual), and responsible entities—an operational M&E policy.

policy_statement: EPA will lead annual assessments of sector implementation of adaptation strategies and report findings to the National Climate Change Steering Committee (NCCSC), alongside ongoing sector evaluations (quarterly and annual).
verbatim_text: As a lead Agency, the EPA will lead and conduct annual assessments on the implementation of the Strategy by the respective sectors and report the findings to NCCSC.
extraction_rationale: Clear commitment assigning EPA to conduct annual assessments and report to NCCSC.

policy_statement: The reporting, monitoring, and review system will support reporting on NAP progress to the UNFCCC Conference of Parties (CoP), and the NAP will be disseminated internationally through the UNFCCC secretariat with regular progress reports via national communications and other channels.
verbatim_text:
The reporting, monitoring, and review will collect information on the progress of NAP implementation... and  provide  outputs  for  the  reporting  on  progress  to  the UNFCCC's Conference of Parties (CoP)...

The NAP would be disseminated internationally and through the UNFCCC secretariat. Regular progress reports would also be submitted to the UNFCCC through existing and new reporting channels, including countries' national communications and submissions.
extraction_rationale: Specifies external reporting and dissemination actions tied to UNFCCC processes—concrete reporting commitments.
